In [1]:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Dynamic Credit-Limit Adjustment & Model-Shock Study — v7.1 Analysis
  Tasks: PfP | Rule/Policy Mapping | Hazard (main) | Extra-Inc & Mechanism
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
"""

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  A. CONFIGURATION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import numpy as np, pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt, seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, GroupKFold
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, brier_score_loss, log_loss, roc_curve
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
import statsmodels.api as sm
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
import xgboost as xgb
import os, itertools
os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"] = "1"

plt.rcParams.update({
    "font.family": "DejaVu Sans", "font.size": 10, "figure.dpi": 150,
    "savefig.dpi": 150, "axes.titlesize": 12, "axes.labelsize": 10,
    "figure.figsize": (9, 5), "legend.fontsize": 8,
})
sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# ── Column name mapping (centralised: edit here if your column names differ) ──
CFG = dict(
    # data paths
    path_2w              = "working_data/2wcase_updated.csv",
    model_path_v4        = "working_data/model/v4-短期.model",
    feature_path_v4      = "working_data/model/v4-短期.xlsx",
    model_path_v5        = "working_data/model/v5-短期.model",
    feature_path_v5      = "working_data/model/v5-短期.xlsx",

    # column names — change here if your data uses different names
    col_user_id          = "user_id",
    col_done_date        = "first_done_date",
    col_bf_credit        = "first_bf_credit",
    col_af_credit        = "first_af_credit",
    col_model_type       = "model_type",
    col_mob1_label       = "mob1_label7",
    col_mob2_label       = "mob2_label7",
    col_repay_date       = "repay_cycle_end_date",

    # analysis parameters
    n_folds              = 5,
    seed                 = 42,
    xgb_device           = "cuda",
    max_followup_days    = 60,
    n_score_bins         = 20,
    n_dml_repeats        = 5,
    sig_threshold        = 0.10,   # significance threshold for evidence flags
)

# ── Global evidence store: all flags populated by real tests, NEVER hardcoded ──
EVIDENCE = {}


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  UTILITY FUNCTIONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def stars(p):
    if p < 0.01: return "***"
    if p < 0.05: return "**"
    if p < 0.10: return "*"
    return ""

def evidence_label(supported, tested=True):
    """Return a standard evidence-tier label."""
    if not tested:
        return "Not tested"
    if supported is None:
        return "Inconclusive"
    return "Supported" if supported else "Not supported"

def stata_table(model, title="", show_or=False):
    """Print regression results in Stata-style format."""
    print(f"\n{'─'*80}")
    if title:
        print(f"  {title}")
    print(f"{'─'*80}")
    hdr = f"  {'Variable':<28s} {'Coef.':>9s} {'Std.Err.':>10s} {'z':>8s} {'P>|z|':>8s}  {'[95% CI]':>20s}"
    if show_or:
        hdr += f"  {'OR':>7s}"
    print(hdr)
    print(f"{'─'*80}")
    ci = model.conf_int()
    for v in model.params.index:
        c  = model.params[v]
        se = model.bse[v]
        z  = c / se if se > 0 else 0
        p  = model.pvalues[v]
        lo, hi = ci.loc[v, 0], ci.loc[v, 1]
        line = f"  {v:<28s} {c:>9.4f} {se:>10.4f} {z:>8.2f} {p:>8.4f}  [{lo:>8.4f}, {hi:>8.4f}] {stars(p)}"
        if show_or:
            line += f"  {np.exp(c):>7.3f}"
        print(line)
    print(f"{'─'*80}")
    nobs = int(model.nobs) if hasattr(model, 'nobs') else "N/A"
    aic  = f"{model.aic:.1f}" if hasattr(model, 'aic') else "N/A"
    print(f"  N = {nobs:,}   AIC = {aic}")
    print("  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01")
    return model

def compute_smd(t, c):
    t, c = pd.Series(t).dropna(), pd.Series(c).dropna()
    if len(t) < 2 or len(c) < 2: return np.nan
    ps = np.sqrt((t.var() + c.var()) / 2)
    return (t.mean() - c.mean()) / ps if ps > 0 else 0

def kaplan_meier(T, E):
    dk = pd.DataFrame({"T": T, "E": E}).dropna()
    ts = sorted(dk["T"].unique())
    nr, s = len(dk), 1.0
    out = [{"time": 0, "survival": 1.0}]
    for t in ts:
        d = ((dk["T"] == t) & (dk["E"] == 1)).sum()
        c_ = ((dk["T"] == t) & (dk["E"] == 0)).sum()
        if nr > 0: s *= (1 - d / nr)
        out.append({"time": t, "survival": s})
        nr -= (d + c_)
    return pd.DataFrame(out)

def safe_qcut_rank(s, q, labels=False):
    r = pd.Series(s).rank(method="first")
    q_eff = min(q, r.nunique())
    if q_eff < 2:
        return pd.Series(np.zeros(len(r), dtype=int), index=r.index)
    return pd.qcut(r, q=q_eff, labels=labels, duplicates="drop")

def rd_ci(y1, y0, alpha=0.05):
    y1, y0 = pd.Series(y1).dropna().astype(float), pd.Series(y0).dropna().astype(float)
    p1, p0 = y1.mean(), y0.mean()
    n1, n0 = len(y1), len(y0)
    se = np.sqrt(p1*(1-p1)/n1 + p0*(1-p0)/n0)
    z = stats.norm.ppf(1 - alpha/2)
    return p1 - p0, p1 - p0 - z*se, p1 - p0 + z*se

def xgb_crossfit(df, feats, target, cfg, group_col="user_id", mcw=5):
    """Cross-fitted XGBoost predictions with grouped CV."""
    feats_use = [f for f in feats if f in df.columns and df[f].notna().any()]
    if len(feats_use) < 3:
        return np.full(len(df), np.nan), np.nan, np.nan, 0
    X = df[feats_use].values.astype(np.float32)
    y = df[target].values.astype(int)
    oof = np.full(len(df), np.nan)
    groups = df[group_col].values if group_col in df.columns else np.arange(len(df))
    gkf = GroupKFold(n_splits=cfg["n_folds"])
    aucs = []
    for tr_idx, te_idx in gkf.split(X, y, groups):
        dtrain = xgb.DMatrix(X[tr_idx], label=y[tr_idx], feature_names=feats_use, missing=np.nan)
        dtest  = xgb.DMatrix(X[te_idx], feature_names=feats_use, missing=np.nan)
        params = {
            "objective": "binary:logistic", "max_depth": 4, "learning_rate": 0.05,
            "subsample": 0.8, "colsample_bytree": 0.6, "min_child_weight": mcw,
            "device": cfg["xgb_device"], "verbosity": 0, "seed": cfg["seed"],
        }
        bst = xgb.train(params, dtrain, num_boost_round=200, verbose_eval=False)
        pred = bst.predict(dtest)
        oof[te_idx] = pred
        if y[te_idx].sum() > 0 and y[te_idx].sum() < len(te_idx):
            aucs.append(roc_auc_score(y[te_idx], pred))
    auc_mean = np.mean(aucs) if aucs else np.nan
    auc_std  = np.std(aucs) if aucs else np.nan
    return oof, auc_mean, auc_std, len(feats_use)

def logrank_test(T1, E1, T2, E2):
    d1 = pd.DataFrame({"T": T1, "E": E1, "g": 0}).dropna()
    d2 = pd.DataFrame({"T": T2, "E": E2, "g": 1}).dropna()
    da = pd.concat([d1, d2])
    ts = sorted(da.loc[da["E"] == 1, "T"].unique())
    O1 = E1e = V = 0
    for t in ts:
        ar = da[da["T"] >= t]; n = len(ar); n1 = (ar["g"] == 0).sum()
        d = ((ar["T"] == t) & (ar["E"] == 1)).sum()
        d1v = ((ar["T"] == t) & (ar["E"] == 1) & (ar["g"] == 0)).sum()
        if n < 2: continue
        e1 = n1 * d / n; O1 += d1v; E1e += e1; V += n1*(n-n1)*d*(n-d) / (n**2*(n-1))
    chi2 = (O1 - E1e)**2 / V if V > 0 else np.nan
    return chi2, 1 - stats.chi2.cdf(chi2, 1) if V > 0 else np.nan


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  B. DATA PREPROCESSING
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("="*80)
print("  SECTION B: DATA LOADING & PREPROCESSING")
print("="*80)

# ── B.1  Basic cleaning ──
def basic_clean(df):
    df = df.copy()
    df["first_done_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df[CFG["col_done_date"]], errors="coerce")
    for c in [CFG["col_bf_credit"], CFG["col_af_credit"]]:
        df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")
    for c in [CFG["col_mob1_label"], CFG["col_mob2_label"]]:
        if c in df.columns:
            df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")
        else:
            df[c] = np.nan
    if CFG["col_repay_date"] in df.columns:
        df[CFG["col_repay_date"]] = pd.to_datetime(df[CFG["col_repay_date"]], errors="coerce")
    df = df.dropna(subset=["first_done_date", CFG["col_bf_credit"], CFG["col_af_credit"]]).copy()
    df["event_date"] = df["first_done_date"].dt.normalize()
    bf = df[CFG["col_bf_credit"]]
    af = df[CFG["col_af_credit"]]
    df["treat_dir"] = np.select(
        [af > bf, af == bf, af < bf], ["inc", "same", "dec"], default="unknown"
    )
    df = df[df["treat_dir"].isin(["inc", "same", "dec"])].copy()
    df["is_inc"]    = (df["treat_dir"] == "inc").astype(int)
    df["is_dec"]    = (df["treat_dir"] == "dec").astype(int)
    df["delta_abs"] = af - bf
    df["delta_rel"] = np.where(bf > 0, (af - bf) / bf, 0.0)
    if CFG["col_model_type"] in df.columns:
        mt = df[CFG["col_model_type"]].astype(str).str.upper()
        df["model_family"] = np.where(mt.str.contains("V4"), "v4",
                             np.where(mt.str.contains("V5"), "v5", "other"))
    else:
        df["model_family"] = "unknown"
    for c in [CFG["col_mob1_label"], CFG["col_mob2_label"]]:
        df[f"{c}_mature"] = df[c].notna().astype(int)
    df["label_any_mature"] = (df[f"{CFG['col_mob1_label']}_mature"] |
                               df[f"{CFG['col_mob2_label']}_mature"]).astype(int)
    df["default"] = np.where(
        df["label_any_mature"] == 1,
        np.fmax(df[CFG["col_mob1_label"]].fillna(0), df[CFG["col_mob2_label"]].fillna(0)),
        np.nan,
    )
    return df

df_2w = basic_clean(pd.read_csv(CFG["path_2w"], low_memory=False))

# ── B.2  Load feature lists & models ──
feat_df_v4 = pd.read_excel(CFG["feature_path_v4"])
feat_df_v5 = pd.read_excel(CFG["feature_path_v5"])

def gl(f):
    return f["索引"].dropna().tolist() if "索引" in f.columns else f.iloc[:, 0].dropna().tolist()

CFG["feat_v4"]    = gl(feat_df_v4)
CFG["feat_v5"]    = gl(feat_df_v5)
CFG["feat_inter"] = sorted(set(CFG["feat_v4"]) & set(CFG["feat_v5"]))
CFG["feat_union"] = sorted(set(CFG["feat_v4"]) | set(CFG["feat_v5"]))

bv4 = xgb.Booster(); bv4.load_model(CFG["model_path_v4"])
bv5 = xgb.Booster(); bv5.load_model(CFG["model_path_v5"])

def score_with_model(df, booster, flist, out_col):
    X = pd.DataFrame(
        {f: pd.to_numeric(df[f], errors="coerce").values if f in df.columns else np.nan for f in flist},
        index=df.index,
    )
    df[out_col] = booster.predict(xgb.DMatrix(X.values, feature_names=flist, missing=np.nan))
    return df

df_2w = score_with_model(df_2w, bv4, CFG["feat_v4"], "score_v4")
df_2w = score_with_model(df_2w, bv5, CFG["feat_v5"], "score_v5")

# ── B.3  Overlap window & continuing users ──
dm = df_2w.groupby(["event_date", "model_family"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
ov = dm.index[(dm.get("v4", pd.Series(dtype=int)) > 0) & (dm.get("v5", pd.Series(dtype=int)) > 0)]
CFG["overlap_start"] = ov.min()
CFG["overlap_end"]   = ov.max()

df_sorted = df_2w.sort_values(["user_id", "event_date", "first_done_date"])
users_pre = set(df_sorted[df_sorted["event_date"] < CFG["overlap_start"]]["user_id"])
users_in  = set(df_sorted[(df_sorted["event_date"] >= CFG["overlap_start"]) &
                           (df_sorted["event_date"] <= CFG["overlap_end"])]["user_id"])
continuing = users_pre & users_in

df_aw = df_sorted[
    (df_sorted["event_date"] >= CFG["overlap_start"]) &
    (df_sorted["event_date"] <= CFG["overlap_end"]) &
    (df_sorted["user_id"].isin(continuing))
].copy()

# ── B.4  Anchor construction ──
anchor = df_aw.loc[df_aw.groupby("user_id")["event_date"].idxmin()].copy()
anchor = anchor.rename(columns={"event_date": "anchor_date"})
df_all = df_sorted[df_sorted["user_id"].isin(continuing)].copy()

first_def = (df_all[df_all["default"] > 0]
             .sort_values("event_date")
             .groupby("user_id")["event_date"].first()
             .reset_index().rename(columns={"event_date": "default_date"}))
last_obs = (df_all.groupby("user_id")["event_date"].max()
            .reset_index().rename(columns={"event_date": "last_date"}))

us = anchor[
    ["user_id", "anchor_date", "model_family", "treat_dir",
     CFG["col_bf_credit"], CFG["col_af_credit"], "score_v4", "score_v5",
     "is_inc", "is_dec", "delta_abs", "delta_rel"]
].merge(first_def, on="user_id", how="left").merge(last_obs, on="user_id", how="left")

us = us[~(us["default_date"].notna() & (us["default_date"] < us["anchor_date"]))].copy()
us["defaulted"] = (us["default_date"].notna() & (us["default_date"] >= us["anchor_date"])).astype(int)
us["T"] = np.where(
    us["defaulted"] == 1,
    (us["default_date"] - us["anchor_date"]).dt.days,
    (us["last_date"] - us["anchor_date"]).dt.days,
).clip(1, CFG["max_followup_days"])
us.loc[(us["defaulted"] == 1) & ((us["default_date"] - us["anchor_date"]).dt.days > CFG["max_followup_days"]),
       "defaulted"] = 0

us["is_v5"]     = (us["model_family"] == "v5").astype(int)
us["gap_raw"]   = us["score_v4"] - us["score_v5"]
us["score_avg"] = (us["score_v4"] + us["score_v5"]) / 2

# ── B.5  Score standardisation: z-scores & percentiles ──
for sc in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
    mu, sd = us[sc].mean(), us[sc].std()
    us[f"{sc}_z"]   = (us[sc] - mu) / sd if sd > 0 else 0.0
    us[f"{sc}_pct"] = us[sc].rank(pct=True)

for mf in ["v4", "v5"]:
    mask = us["model_family"] == mf
    own_score = f"score_{mf}"
    mu, sd = us.loc[mask, own_score].mean(), us.loc[mask, own_score].std()
    us.loc[mask, "own_score_within_z"]   = (us.loc[mask, own_score] - mu) / sd if sd > 0 else 0.0
    us.loc[mask, "own_score_within_pct"] = us.loc[mask, own_score].rank(pct=True)
    us.loc[mask, "own_score"]            = us.loc[mask, own_score]

# ── B.6  Credit standardisation ──
credit_mu = us[CFG["col_bf_credit"]].mean()
credit_sd = us[CFG["col_bf_credit"]].std()
us["lc_z"] = (us[CFG["col_bf_credit"]] - credit_mu) / credit_sd if credit_sd > 0 else 0.0
us["delta_abs_z"] = (us["delta_abs"] - us["delta_abs"].mean()) / us["delta_abs"].std() if us["delta_abs"].std() > 0 else 0.0

# ── B.7  Merge HD features to user-level ──
union_feats = [f for f in CFG["feat_union"] if f in anchor.columns]
us = us.merge(
    anchor[["user_id"] + union_feats].drop_duplicates("user_id"),
    on="user_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_x"),
)

feat_inter_us = [f for f in CFG["feat_inter"] if f in us.columns and us[f].notna().any()]
risk_oof_us, risk_auc_us, risk_std_us, n_feat_risk = xgb_crossfit(
    us, feat_inter_us, "defaulted", CFG, group_col="user_id", mcw=10
)
us["risk_ml"] = risk_oof_us

print(f"  Overlap window : {CFG['overlap_start']} to {CFG['overlap_end']}")
print(f"  Users          : {len(us)}, V4={(us['is_v5']==0).sum()}, V5={(us['is_v5']==1).sum()}")
print(f"  Defaults       : {us['defaulted'].sum()} ({us['defaulted'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Treatment      : inc={(us['treat_dir']=='inc').sum()}, same={(us['treat_dir']=='same').sum()}, dec={(us['treat_dir']=='dec').sum()}")
print(f"  User-level risk score (X_inter, {n_feat_risk} features): AUC={risk_auc_us:.4f}±{risk_std_us:.4f}")

# ── B.8  Document m(X) definition ──
print(f"\n  ── m(X) definition for all subsequent PfP / control analyses ──")
print(f"  m(X) = risk_ml: a cross-fitted XGBoost prediction of 'defaulted'")
print(f"         using {n_feat_risk} features from feat_inter (V4 ∩ V5 feature set),")
print(f"         trained via {CFG['n_folds']}-fold GroupKFold (grouped by user_id).")
print(f"         OOF AUC = {risk_auc_us:.4f}±{risk_std_us:.4f}")
print(f"  This is the HIGH-DIMENSIONAL baseline control used throughout.")
print(f"  When we say 'controlling for m(X)', we mean including risk_ml as a covariate.")
print(f"  risk_ml captures the observable X's predictive content for default,")
print(f"  but it is NOT the same as the V4/V5 model scores (score_v4, score_v5).")
print(f"  The PfP test asks: after absorbing m(X), do score_v4/v5 still add signal?")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  C. TASK 1: PERFORMATIVE PREDICTION (PfP) TEST
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("  TASK 1: PERFORMATIVE PREDICTION (PfP) TEST")
print("  Goal: Does model score carry additional predictive signal beyond m(X)?")
print("="*80)
print(f"""
  ── What is m(X) here? ──
  m(X) = risk_ml, a cross-fitted XGBoost model of default using {n_feat_risk}
  HD features from the V4 ∩ V5 intersection feature set.
  OOF AUC = {risk_auc_us:.4f}.  It represents the best prediction of default
  achievable from the observable covariates X alone.

  The PfP question is: after controlling for m(X) + baseline credit (lc_z),
  does the V4 or V5 model score still carry significant residual signal
  for default?  If yes, the score contains content NOT fully absorbed by X,
  which is consistent with (but does not prove) performative / policy-feedback.
""")

# ── C.1  GLM / Logistic Regression ──
print("─"*80)
print("  C.1  GLM Logistic Regression: score → default | m(X)")
print("  NOTE: All models below control for m(X) = risk_ml + lc_z.")
print("─"*80)

m_base = smf.glm("defaulted ~ risk_ml + lc_z",
                  data=us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml"]),
                  family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
stata_table(m_base, "Model PfP-0: defaulted ~ risk_ml + lc_z  [Baseline: m(X) only, no score]")

m_pfp1 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v4_z + risk_ml + lc_z",
                  data=us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml"]),
                  family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
stata_table(m_pfp1, "Model PfP-1: defaulted ~ score_v4_z + m(X)  [Add V4 score to baseline]")

m_pfp2 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v5_z + risk_ml + lc_z",
                  data=us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml"]),
                  family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
stata_table(m_pfp2, "Model PfP-2: defaulted ~ score_v5_z + m(X)  [Add V5 score to baseline]")

m_pfp3 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + risk_ml + lc_z",
                  data=us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml"]),
                  family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
stata_table(m_pfp3, "Model PfP-3: defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + m(X)  [Both scores]")

m_pfp4 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + score_v4_z:is_v5 + score_v5_z:is_v5 + is_v5 + risk_ml + lc_z",
                  data=us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml"]),
                  family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
stata_table(m_pfp4, "Model PfP-4: + score × regime interactions + m(X)")

us_v4 = us[us["is_v5"] == 0].dropna(subset=["risk_ml"])
if len(us_v4) > 30:
    m_pfp5 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + risk_ml + lc_z",
                      data=us_v4, family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
    stata_table(m_pfp5, "Model PfP-5: Subsample Anchor=V4 only + m(X)")

us_v5 = us[us["is_v5"] == 1].dropna(subset=["risk_ml"])
if len(us_v5) > 30:
    m_pfp6 = smf.glm("defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + risk_ml + lc_z",
                      data=us_v5, family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
    stata_table(m_pfp6, "Model PfP-6: Subsample Anchor=V5 only + m(X)")

# ── C.2  DML / Partialling-out style estimation ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  C.2  DML / Partialling-out: residualised score → residualised default")
print("  Procedure: E[Y|X] via risk_ml; E[score|X] via cross-fitted XGB regression;")
print("  then OLS of Y-residual on score-residual.")
print("─"*80)

dml_results = {}
for score_name in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
    dml_thetas = []
    us_dml = us.dropna(subset=["risk_ml", score_name]).copy()
    for rep in range(CFG["n_dml_repeats"]):
        np.random.seed(CFG["seed"] + rep)
        X_feats = feat_inter_us[:min(50, len(feat_inter_us))]
        X_use = [f for f in X_feats if f in us_dml.columns and us_dml[f].notna().mean() > 0.5]
        if len(X_use) < 5:
            print(f"    WARNING: too few features for DML ({len(X_use)}), skipping.")
            break
        score_vals = us_dml[score_name].values
        oof_score_pred = np.full(len(us_dml), np.nan)
        gkf = GroupKFold(n_splits=CFG["n_folds"])
        X_arr = us_dml[X_use].values.astype(np.float32)
        groups = us_dml["user_id"].values
        for tr_idx, te_idx in gkf.split(X_arr, score_vals, groups):
            dtrain = xgb.DMatrix(X_arr[tr_idx], label=score_vals[tr_idx],
                                 feature_names=X_use, missing=np.nan)
            dtest  = xgb.DMatrix(X_arr[te_idx], feature_names=X_use, missing=np.nan)
            params = {"objective": "reg:squarederror", "max_depth": 4, "learning_rate": 0.05,
                      "subsample": 0.8, "colsample_bytree": 0.6, "min_child_weight": 10,
                      "device": CFG["xgb_device"], "verbosity": 0, "seed": CFG["seed"] + rep}
            bst = xgb.train(params, dtrain, num_boost_round=200, verbose_eval=False)
            oof_score_pred[te_idx] = bst.predict(dtest)
        y_resid     = us_dml["defaulted"].values - us_dml["risk_ml"].values
        score_resid = score_vals - oof_score_pred
        valid = ~(np.isnan(y_resid) | np.isnan(score_resid))
        X_ols = sm.add_constant(score_resid[valid])
        ols = sm.OLS(y_resid[valid], X_ols).fit(cov_type="HC1")
        dml_thetas.append({"rep": rep, "theta": ols.params[1], "se": ols.bse[1], "p": ols.pvalues[1]})

    if dml_thetas:
        dml_df = pd.DataFrame(dml_thetas)
        med_theta = dml_df["theta"].median()
        med_se    = dml_df["se"].median()
        cauchy_stat = np.mean(np.tan((0.5 - dml_df["p"].values) * np.pi))
        cauchy_p    = 0.5 - np.arctan(cauchy_stat) / np.pi
        dml_results[score_name] = {"theta": med_theta, "se": med_se, "cauchy_p": cauchy_p}
        print(f"\n  DML result for {score_name} (controlling for m(X) via partialling-out):")
        print(f"  {'─'*60}")
        print(f"  {'Rep':<6} {'Theta':>10} {'SE':>10} {'p':>10}")
        print(f"  {'─'*60}")
        for _, row in dml_df.iterrows():
            print(f"  {int(row['rep']):<6} {row['theta']:>10.5f} {row['se']:>10.5f} {row['p']:>10.4f} {stars(row['p'])}")
        print(f"  {'─'*60}")
        print(f"  Median theta = {med_theta:.5f}, Median SE = {med_se:.5f}")
        print(f"  Cauchy-combined p = {cauchy_p:.4f} {stars(cauchy_p)}")

# ── C.3  PfP Summary ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  C.3  PfP Summary Table")
print("  All models control for m(X) = risk_ml (cross-fitted HD risk score) + lc_z")
print("─"*80)
print(f"  {'Model':<40} {'score_v4_z':>12} {'score_v5_z':>12} {'AIC':>10}")
print(f"  {'─'*76}")
for name, m in [("PfP-0: m(X) only [baseline]", m_base),
                ("PfP-1: + v4_score | m(X)", m_pfp1),
                ("PfP-2: + v5_score | m(X)", m_pfp2),
                ("PfP-3: + both scores | m(X)", m_pfp3),
                ("PfP-4: + score × regime | m(X)", m_pfp4)]:
    sv4 = f"{m.params.get('score_v4_z', np.nan):+.4f}{stars(m.pvalues.get('score_v4_z', 1))}" if 'score_v4_z' in m.params else "        —"
    sv5 = f"{m.params.get('score_v5_z', np.nan):+.4f}{stars(m.pvalues.get('score_v5_z', 1))}" if 'score_v5_z' in m.params else "        —"
    print(f"  {name:<40} {sv4:>12} {sv5:>12} {m.aic:>10.1f}")

# Store PfP evidence
pfp_v4_sig = m_pfp3.pvalues.get('score_v4_z', 1) < CFG["sig_threshold"]
pfp_v5_sig = m_pfp3.pvalues.get('score_v5_z', 1) < CFG["sig_threshold"]
EVIDENCE["pfp_v4_residual_signal"] = pfp_v4_sig
EVIDENCE["pfp_v5_residual_signal"] = pfp_v5_sig
EVIDENCE["pfp_any_residual_signal"] = pfp_v4_sig or pfp_v5_sig

print(f"\n  PfP evidence flags (derived from PfP-3, controlling for m(X)):")
print(f"    score_v4 carries residual signal beyond m(X): {evidence_label(pfp_v4_sig)}")
print(f"    score_v5 carries residual signal beyond m(X): {evidence_label(pfp_v5_sig)}")
print(f"\n  Interpretation:")
print(f"  - 'Controlling for m(X)' means: risk_ml (cross-fitted XGB, {n_feat_risk} features) + lc_z")
print(f"  - If score is significant here, it means score contains signal that the")
print(f"    observable X alone cannot reproduce.  This is consistent with PfP content")
print(f"    (score carries information about treatment/policy, not just risk).")
print(f"  - This does NOT establish causal PfP.  It only shows the score is not")
print(f"    a pure sufficient statistic of X for predicting default.")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  D. TASK 2: RULE / POLICY MAPPING TEST
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("  TASK 2: RULE / POLICY MAPPING TEST")
print("  Goal: Given same 'score position', does P(inc) differ across V4/V5?")
print("="*80)

# ── D.1  Score distribution comparison ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.1  Score Distribution Comparison: V4 vs V5")
print("─"*80)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))

for i, (sc, lbl) in enumerate([("score_v4", "V4 Score"), ("score_v5", "V5 Score")]):
    ax = axes[0, i]
    for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
        sub = us[us["model_family"] == mf][sc].dropna()
        ax.hist(sub, bins=40, alpha=0.4, density=True, color=c, label=f"{mf} (n={len(sub)})")
        ax.axvline(sub.mean(), color=c, ls="--", lw=2)
    ax.set(xlabel=lbl, ylabel="Density", title=f"{lbl} Distribution by Regime")
    ax.legend()

ax = axes[0, 2]
for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
    sub = us[us["model_family"] == mf]["own_score_within_z"].dropna()
    ax.hist(sub, bins=40, alpha=0.4, density=True, color=c, label=f"{mf} within-z (n={len(sub)})")
    ax.axvline(sub.mean(), color=c, ls="--", lw=2)
ax.set(xlabel="Within-model Z-score", ylabel="Density", title="Own-score Within-model Z-score")
ax.legend()

ax = axes[1, 0]
data_box = []
for mf in ["v4", "v5"]:
    sub = us[us["model_family"] == mf]
    for sc in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
        for val in sub[sc].dropna():
            data_box.append({"regime": mf, "score_type": sc, "value": val})
bp_df = pd.DataFrame(data_box)
if len(bp_df) > 0:
    sns.boxplot(data=bp_df, x="score_type", y="value", hue="regime", ax=ax, palette=["C0", "C1"])
    ax.set(title="Score Boxplot by Regime")

ax = axes[1, 1]
quantiles = np.arange(0.05, 1.0, 0.05)
for sc, c, mk in [("score_v4", "C0", "o"), ("score_v5", "C1", "s")]:
    q_v4 = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v4", sc].quantile(quantiles).values
    q_v5 = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v5", sc].quantile(quantiles).values
    ax.plot(q_v4, q_v5, f"{mk}-", color=c, ms=4, label=f"{sc}", alpha=0.7)
ax.plot([0, 0.5], [0, 0.5], "k--", alpha=0.3)
ax.set(xlabel="V4-regime quantile", ylabel="V5-regime quantile", title="QQ Plot: V4 vs V5 Regime Score Quantiles")
ax.legend()

ax = axes[1, 2]
ks_results = []
score_shift_tests = {}  # store for evidence flags
for sc in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
    v4_vals = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v4", sc].dropna()
    v5_vals = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v5", sc].dropna()
    ks_stat, ks_p = stats.ks_2samp(v4_vals, v5_vals)
    t_stat, t_p   = stats.ttest_ind(v4_vals, v5_vals)
    ks_results.append({"score": sc, "KS_stat": ks_stat, "KS_p": ks_p})
    score_shift_tests[sc] = {"ks_p": ks_p, "t_p": t_p, "t_stat": t_stat,
                              "v4_mean": v4_vals.mean(), "v5_mean": v5_vals.mean(),
                              "diff": v5_vals.mean() - v4_vals.mean()}
    for vals, lbl, c in [(v4_vals, f"V4 {sc}", "C0"), (v5_vals, f"V5 {sc}", "C1")]:
        sorted_v = np.sort(vals)
        cdf = np.arange(1, len(sorted_v)+1) / len(sorted_v)
        ls = "-" if sc == "score_v4" else "--"
        ax.plot(sorted_v, cdf, color=c, ls=ls, lw=1.5, label=lbl, alpha=0.7)
ax.set(xlabel="Score", ylabel="CDF", title="CDF Comparison")
ax.legend(fontsize=7)

fig.suptitle("Task 2: Score Distribution Comparison — V4 vs V5 Regime", fontsize=14)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\n  Score distribution tests:")
print(f"  {'Score':<12} {'KS stat':>10} {'KS p':>10} {'t stat':>10} {'t p':>10} {'V4 mean':>10} {'V5 mean':>10} {'Diff':>10}")
print(f"  {'─'*82}")
for sc in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
    r = score_shift_tests[sc]
    print(f"  {sc:<12} {r['ks_p']:.4f}      {r['ks_p']:.4f}      {r['t_stat']:>+8.2f}   {r['t_p']:.4f}"
          f"     {r['v4_mean']:.4f}     {r['v5_mean']:.4f}   {r['diff']:+.4f} {stars(r['t_p'])}")

# ── Populate score distribution shift evidence from actual tests ──
EVIDENCE["score_v4_distribution_shift"] = score_shift_tests["score_v4"]["ks_p"] < CFG["sig_threshold"]
EVIDENCE["score_v5_distribution_shift"] = score_shift_tests["score_v5"]["ks_p"] < CFG["sig_threshold"]
EVIDENCE["any_score_distribution_shift"] = (EVIDENCE["score_v4_distribution_shift"] or
                                             EVIDENCE["score_v5_distribution_shift"])

print(f"\n  Evidence flags (from actual KS + t-tests, threshold p < {CFG['sig_threshold']}):")
print(f"    V4-score distribution differs across regimes: {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['score_v4_distribution_shift'])}")
print(f"    V5-score distribution differs across regimes: {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['score_v5_distribution_shift'])}")

# ── D.2  P(inc | score_position, regime) comparison ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.2  P(inc | score_position, regime) — Three Scales")
print("─"*80)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
scale_names = ["Raw Score (score_v4)", "Pooled Z-score (score_v4_z)", "Pooled Percentile (score_v4_pct)"]
scale_cols  = ["score_v4", "score_v4_z", "score_v4_pct"]

for ax_i, (scale_col, scale_name) in enumerate(zip(scale_cols, scale_names)):
    ax = axes[ax_i]
    us["_bin"] = safe_qcut_rank(us[scale_col], q=CFG["n_score_bins"], labels=False)
    mapping_rows = []
    for b in sorted(us["_bin"].dropna().unique()):
        sub = us[us["_bin"] == b]
        for mf in ["v4", "v5"]:
            s = sub[sub["model_family"] == mf]
            if len(s) < 3: continue
            mapping_rows.append({
                "bin": b, "x_mean": sub[scale_col].mean(), "model": mf,
                "inc_rate": s["is_inc"].mean(), "n": len(s),
            })
    mdf = pd.DataFrame(mapping_rows)
    for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
        sub = mdf[mdf["model"] == mf]
        ax.plot(sub["x_mean"], sub["inc_rate"], "o-", color=c, label=f"P(inc|{mf})", ms=4, lw=1.5)
    ax.set(xlabel=scale_name, ylabel="P(inc)", title=f"Inc Rate by {scale_name}")
    ax.legend()

fig.suptitle("Task 2: P(inc | score position, regime) — Three Scales", fontsize=14)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── D.3  Logistic regression with EXPLICIT slope vs level decomposition ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.3  Logistic: inc ~ score + regime + score × regime")
print("  Decomposition: regime main effect = LEVEL SHIFT")
print("                 interaction         = SLOPE DIFFERENCE")
print("─"*80)

# Store all mapping regression results for later evidence extraction
mapping_regressions = {}

for scale_col, scale_label in [("score_v4_z", "V4 Z-score"),
                                 ("score_v4_pct", "V4 Percentile"),
                                 ("own_score_within_z", "Within-model Z-score")]:
    if scale_col not in us.columns: continue
    us_tmp = us.dropna(subset=[scale_col]).copy()
    us_tmp["_sc"] = us_tmp[scale_col]
    m = smf.glm("is_inc ~ _sc + is_v5 + _sc:is_v5",
                data=us_tmp, family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(cov_type="HC1")
    stata_table(m, f"Rule Mapping [{scale_label}]: inc ~ score + regime + interaction")
    mapping_regressions[scale_label] = m

    # Extract and report level vs slope
    level_coef = m.params.get("is_v5", np.nan)
    level_p    = m.pvalues.get("is_v5", np.nan)
    slope_coef = m.params.get("_sc:is_v5", np.nan)
    slope_p    = m.pvalues.get("_sc:is_v5", np.nan)
    print(f"  ── Decomposition for {scale_label} ──")
    print(f"     Level shift (regime main effect is_v5):  coef={level_coef:+.4f}, p={level_p:.4f} {stars(level_p)}")
    print(f"     Slope difference (score × regime):       coef={slope_coef:+.4f}, p={slope_p:.4f} {stars(slope_p)}")
    if level_p < CFG["sig_threshold"]:
        print(f"     → Evidence of LEVEL SHIFT: at the same score position, V5 has a")
        print(f"       {'higher' if level_coef > 0 else 'lower'} baseline P(inc), regardless of score.")
    else:
        print(f"     → No significant level shift detected at p < {CFG['sig_threshold']}.")
    if slope_p < CFG["sig_threshold"]:
        print(f"     → Evidence of SLOPE DIFFERENCE: the score-inc mapping has")
        print(f"       a different gradient under V5.")
    else:
        print(f"     → No significant slope difference detected at p < {CFG['sig_threshold']}.")

# ── Use the V4-percentile scale as the primary mapping test ──
m_rule_primary = mapping_regressions.get("V4 Percentile", None)
if m_rule_primary is None:
    # fallback
    m_rule_primary = list(mapping_regressions.values())[0] if mapping_regressions else None

if m_rule_primary is not None:
    rule_level_coef = m_rule_primary.params.get("is_v5", np.nan)
    rule_level_p    = m_rule_primary.pvalues.get("is_v5", np.nan)
    rule_slope_coef = m_rule_primary.params.get("_sc:is_v5", np.nan)
    rule_slope_p    = m_rule_primary.pvalues.get("_sc:is_v5", np.nan)

    EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"]       = rule_level_p < CFG["sig_threshold"]
    EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]  = rule_slope_p < CFG["sig_threshold"]
    EVIDENCE["mapping_any_change"]        = EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"] or EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]
else:
    EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"]       = None
    EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]  = None
    EVIDENCE["mapping_any_change"]        = None

# ── D.4  Calibration check ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.4  Calibration Check: Actual Default Rate by Score Bin × Regime")
print("─"*80)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
calibration_shifts = {}

for ax_i, (sc, title_sc) in enumerate([("score_v4", "V4 Score"), ("score_v5", "V5 Score")]):
    ax = axes[ax_i]
    us["_cal_bin"] = safe_qcut_rank(us[sc], q=10, labels=False)
    cal_rows = []
    for b in sorted(us["_cal_bin"].dropna().unique()):
        sub = us[us["_cal_bin"] == b]
        for mf in ["v4", "v5"]:
            s = sub[sub["model_family"] == mf]
            if len(s) < 5: continue
            cal_rows.append({
                "bin": b, "pred_mean": s[sc].mean(), "actual_dr": s["defaulted"].mean(),
                "model": mf, "n": len(s),
            })
    cdf_cal = pd.DataFrame(cal_rows)

    # Compute calibration gap: in each bin where both models present,
    # does V5 show systematically different actual default rate for same predicted score?
    cal_gaps = []
    for b in cdf_cal["bin"].unique():
        v4_sub = cdf_cal[(cdf_cal["bin"] == b) & (cdf_cal["model"] == "v4")]
        v5_sub = cdf_cal[(cdf_cal["bin"] == b) & (cdf_cal["model"] == "v5")]
        if len(v4_sub) == 1 and len(v5_sub) == 1:
            cal_gaps.append(v5_sub["actual_dr"].iloc[0] - v4_sub["actual_dr"].iloc[0])
    if cal_gaps:
        mean_cal_gap = np.mean(cal_gaps)
        calibration_shifts[sc] = mean_cal_gap

    for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
        sub = cdf_cal[cdf_cal["model"] == mf]
        ax.plot(sub["pred_mean"], sub["actual_dr"], "o-", color=c, label=f"{mf}", ms=5)
    ax.plot([0, 0.5], [0, 0.5], "k--", alpha=0.3)
    ax.set(xlabel=f"Mean {title_sc}", ylabel="Actual Default Rate",
           title=f"Calibration: {title_sc} vs Actual Default")
    ax.legend()

fig.suptitle("Task 2: Calibration — Score Prediction vs Actual Default Rate", fontsize=14)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Calibration shift evidence
for sc, gap in calibration_shifts.items():
    print(f"  Mean calibration gap ({sc}): V5 - V4 actual DR at same score bin = {gap:+.4f}")

EVIDENCE["score_calibration_shift"] = any(abs(g) > 0.01 for g in calibration_shifts.values()) if calibration_shifts else None

# ── D.5  Within-percentile inc-rate comparison ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.5  Within-Percentile Inc Rate Comparison")
print("─"*80)

us["_pct_bin"] = safe_qcut_rank(us["own_score_within_pct"], q=10, labels=False)
pct_rows = []
for b in sorted(us["_pct_bin"].dropna().unique()):
    sub = us[us["_pct_bin"] == b]
    for mf in ["v4", "v5"]:
        s = sub[sub["model_family"] == mf]
        if len(s) < 3: continue
        pct_rows.append({"bin": b, "pct_mean": s["own_score_within_pct"].mean(),
                         "model": mf, "inc_rate": s["is_inc"].mean(), "n": len(s)})
pdf = pd.DataFrame(pct_rows)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
    sub = pdf[pdf["model"] == mf]
    ax.plot(sub["pct_mean"], sub["inc_rate"], "o-", color=c, label=f"P(inc|{mf})", ms=5)
ax.set(xlabel="Within-model Score Percentile", ylabel="P(inc)",
       title="Task 2: Inc Rate by Own-model Percentile Position")
ax.legend()
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── D.6  Rule Mapping Conclusion ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  D.6  Rule Mapping Evidence Summary")
print("─"*80)

inc_v4 = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v4", "is_inc"].mean()
inc_v5 = us.loc[us["model_family"] == "v5", "is_inc"].mean()
EVIDENCE["v5_higher_inc_rate"] = inc_v5 > inc_v4  # simple descriptive fact

print(f"  Inc rate: V4={inc_v4:.1%}, V5={inc_v5:.1%}, diff={inc_v5-inc_v4:+.1%}")
print(f"\n  ── Evidence Summary (Task 2) ──")
print(f"  Score distribution shift:       {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['any_score_distribution_shift'])}")
print(f"    (V4 score across regimes: KS p={score_shift_tests['score_v4']['ks_p']:.4f})")
print(f"    (V5 score across regimes: KS p={score_shift_tests['score_v5']['ks_p']:.4f})")
print(f"  Calibration shift:              {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('score_calibration_shift'))}")
print(f"  Mapping level shift (is_v5):    {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['mapping_level_shift'])}")
if m_rule_primary is not None:
    print(f"    (coef={rule_level_coef:+.4f}, p={rule_level_p:.4f})")
print(f"  Mapping slope difference:       {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['mapping_slope_difference'])}")
if m_rule_primary is not None:
    print(f"    (coef={rule_slope_coef:+.4f}, p={rule_slope_p:.4f})")
print(f"  V5 has higher inc rate:         {'Yes' if EVIDENCE['v5_higher_inc_rate'] else 'No'}")

print(f"\n  CRITICAL NOTE on interpretation:")
print(f"  - 'Interaction not significant' does NOT mean 'rule mapping is stable'.")
print(f"  - If the level shift (regime main effect) IS significant, the mapping")
print(f"    has changed even if the slope is the same: P(inc) is shifted up/down")
print(f"    at every score position.")
print(f"  - To claim 'mapping stable', BOTH level AND slope must be non-significant.")
if EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"] and not EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]:
    print(f"  → Current evidence: no slope reshaping, but there IS a level shift.")
    print(f"    This means the rule mapping has changed (parallel upward shift),")
    print(f"    even though the score-to-inc gradient is similar across regimes.")
elif EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"] and EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]:
    print(f"  → Current evidence: BOTH level shift AND slope change detected.")
    print(f"    The rule mapping has changed in both intercept and gradient.")
elif not EVIDENCE["mapping_level_shift"] and not EVIDENCE["mapping_slope_difference"]:
    print(f"  → Current evidence: neither level shift nor slope change significant.")
    print(f"    This is consistent with (but does not prove) mapping stability.")
else:
    print(f"  → Current evidence: slope change without level shift — unusual pattern.")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  E. TASK 3: HAZARD MAIN ANALYSIS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("  TASK 3: HAZARD MAIN ANALYSIS — Discrete-Time Pooled Logistic")
print("  IMPORTANT: Sample = selected continuing users. Do NOT extrapolate.")
print("="*80)

# ── E.1  Counting Process Construction with Cumulative Exposure ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  E.1  Counting Process Construction")
print("─"*80)

anchor_dates = anchor[["user_id", "anchor_date", "model_family"]].copy()
ep = df_all.merge(anchor_dates, on="user_id", how="inner", suffixes=("", "_anc"))
ep["day"] = (ep["event_date"] - ep["anchor_date"]).dt.days
ep = ep[(ep["day"] >= 0) & (ep["day"] <= CFG["max_followup_days"])].copy()
ep["event_default"] = (ep["default"] > 0).astype(int)
ep = ep.sort_values(["user_id", "day", "first_done_date"])

EPS = 0.01
cp_rows = []

for uid, grp in ep.groupby("user_id"):
    grp = grp.sort_values(["day", "first_done_date"]).reset_index(drop=True)
    n = len(grp)
    eff = np.empty(n, dtype=float)
    for idx in range(n):
        d = grp.iloc[idx]["day"]
        K = (grp["day"] == d).sum()
        k = (grp["day"] == d).iloc[:idx+1].sum() - 1
        eff[idx] = d + (k + 1) / (K + 1)
    for idx in range(n):
        eff[idx] = max(eff[idx], EPS) if idx == 0 else max(eff[idx], eff[idx-1] + EPS)
    base_credit = max(grp.iloc[0][CFG["col_bf_credit"]], 1.0)
    cum_pos = cum_neg = cum_net = 0.0
    dose_pos = dose_neg = dose_net = 0.0
    prev_day = 0
    for i in range(n):
        row = grp.iloc[i]
        start = eff[i-1] if i > 0 else 0.0
        stop  = eff[i]
        ev    = int(row["event_default"] == 1)
        delta = row["delta_abs"]
        delta_rel = delta / base_credit
        if delta > 0: cum_pos += delta_rel
        elif delta < 0: cum_neg += abs(delta_rel)
        cum_net += delta_rel
        dt = max(row["day"] - prev_day, 1) if i > 0 else 1
        if delta > 0: dose_pos += delta_rel * dt
        elif delta < 0: dose_neg += abs(delta_rel) * dt
        dose_net += delta_rel * dt
        prev_day = row["day"]
        cp_rows.append({
            "user_id": uid, "interval_idx": i, "start": start, "stop": stop,
            "event": ev, "is_v5": int(row["model_family_anc"] == "v5"),
            "tv_is_inc": int(row["is_inc"]), "tv_is_dec": int(row["is_dec"]),
            "tv_score_v4": row["score_v4"], "tv_score_v5": row["score_v5"],
            "delta_rel": delta_rel,
            "cum_pos_rel": cum_pos, "cum_neg_rel": cum_neg, "cum_net_rel": cum_net,
            "cum_abs_rel": cum_pos + cum_neg,
            "dose_pos_rel": dose_pos, "dose_neg_rel": dose_neg, "dose_net_rel": dose_net,
            "dose_abs_rel": abs(dose_pos) + abs(dose_neg),
            "base_credit": base_credit,
        })
        if ev == 1: break

cp = pd.DataFrame(cp_rows)
cp["t_mid"]    = (cp["start"] + cp["stop"]) / 2
cp["t_mid_sq"] = cp["t_mid"] ** 2

for col in ["cum_pos_rel", "cum_neg_rel", "cum_net_rel", "cum_abs_rel",
            "dose_pos_rel", "dose_neg_rel", "dose_net_rel", "dose_abs_rel"]:
    mu, sd = cp[col].mean(), cp[col].std()
    cp[f"{col}_z"] = (cp[col] - mu) / sd if sd > 0 else 0.0

bc_mu, bc_sd = cp["base_credit"].mean(), cp["base_credit"].std()
cp["lc_z"] = (cp["base_credit"] - bc_mu) / bc_sd if bc_sd > 0 else 0.0

feat_inter_in_ep = [f for f in CFG["feat_inter"] if f in ep.columns and ep[f].notna().any()]
ep_feats = ep[["user_id"] + feat_inter_in_ep].copy()
ep_feats["_rank"] = ep_feats.groupby("user_id").cumcount()
cp["_rank"] = cp.groupby("user_id").cumcount()
cp = cp.merge(ep_feats, on=["user_id", "_rank"], how="left", suffixes=("", "_ep"))
feat_in_cp = [f for f in feat_inter_in_ep if f in cp.columns]

risk_oof_cp, risk_auc_cp, risk_std_cp, _ = xgb_crossfit(
    cp, feat_in_cp, "event", CFG, group_col="user_id"
)
cp["risk_ml"] = risk_oof_cp

for c in ["tv_score_v4", "tv_score_v5", "risk_ml"]:
    if c in cp.columns:
        cp[c] = cp[c].fillna(cp[c].median())

for sc in ["tv_score_v4", "tv_score_v5"]:
    mu, sd = cp[sc].mean(), cp[sc].std()
    cp[f"{sc}_z"] = (cp[sc] - mu) / sd if sd > 0 else 0.0

print(f"  CP: {len(cp)} intervals, {cp['user_id'].nunique()} users, {cp['event'].sum()} defaults")
print(f"  Event-level risk score AUC: {risk_auc_cp:.4f}±{risk_std_cp:.4f}")

# ── E.2  KM & Log-rank ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  E.2  KM Survival Curves & Log-rank Test")
print("─"*80)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
for mf, c in [("v4", "C0"), ("v5", "C1")]:
    s = us[us["model_family"] == mf]
    km = kaplan_meier(s["T"], s["defaulted"])
    axes[0].step(km["time"], km["survival"], where="post", lw=2, color=c,
                 label=f"{mf} (n={len(s)}, d={s['defaulted'].sum()}, DR={s['defaulted'].mean():.1%})")
axes[0].set(xlabel="Days", ylabel="S(t)", title="KM: V4 vs V5"); axes[0].legend()

for td, c in [("inc", "C1"), ("same", "C2"), ("dec", "C3")]:
    s = us[us["treat_dir"] == td]; km = kaplan_meier(s["T"], s["defaulted"])
    axes[1].step(km["time"], km["survival"], where="post", lw=2, color=c,
                 label=f"{td} (n={len(s)}, DR={s['defaulted'].mean():.1%})")
axes[1].set(xlabel="Days", ylabel="S(t)", title="KM: By Treatment Direction"); axes[1].legend()

for mf, ls in [("v4", "-"), ("v5", "--")]:
    for td, c in [("inc", "C1"), ("same", "C2"), ("dec", "C3")]:
        s = us[(us["model_family"] == mf) & (us["treat_dir"] == td)]
        if len(s) < 10: continue
        km = kaplan_meier(s["T"], s["defaulted"])
        axes[2].step(km["time"], km["survival"], where="post", lw=1.5, color=c, ls=ls,
                     label=f"{mf}-{td} ({s['defaulted'].mean():.0%})")
axes[2].set(xlabel="Days", ylabel="S(t)", title="KM: Model × Treatment")
axes[2].legend(fontsize=6, ncol=2)
fig.suptitle("Task 3: Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves", fontsize=14)
fig.tight_layout(); plt.show()

s4 = us[us["is_v5"] == 0]; s5 = us[us["is_v5"] == 1]
lr_chi2, lr_p = logrank_test(s4["T"], s4["defaulted"], s5["T"], s5["defaulted"])
print(f"  Log-rank V4 vs V5: chi2={lr_chi2:.3f}, p={lr_p:.4f} {stars(lr_p)}")

# ── E.3  Progressive Hazard Specifications ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  E.3  Progressive Hazard Model Specifications")
print("─"*80)

hazard_models = {}
hazard_specs = [
    ("H1: time + regime",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5"),
    ("H2: + event-level treatment",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec"),
    ("H3: + HD risk control",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml"),
    ("H4: + score controls",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml + tv_score_v4_z + tv_score_v5_z"),
    ("H5: + cumulative exposure",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml + cum_pos_rel_z + cum_neg_rel_z"),
    ("H6: + dose exposure",
     "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml + dose_pos_rel_z + dose_neg_rel_z"),
]

for spec_name, formula in hazard_specs:
    vars_needed = [v.strip() for v in formula.split("~")[1].replace(":", "+").split("+")]
    vars_needed = [v.strip() for v in vars_needed if v.strip()]
    missing = [v for v in vars_needed if v not in cp.columns]
    if missing:
        print(f"\n  SKIP {spec_name}: missing columns {missing}")
        continue
    try:
        cp_clean = cp.dropna(subset=[v for v in vars_needed if v in cp.columns])
        m = smf.glm(formula, data=cp_clean, family=sm.families.Binomial()).fit(
            cov_type="cluster", cov_kwds={"groups": cp_clean["user_id"]}
        )
        hazard_models[spec_name] = m
        stata_table(m, spec_name, show_or=True)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"\n  ERROR in {spec_name}: {e}")

# ── E.4  Hazard Summary Table ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  E.4  Hazard Coefficient Summary (Key Variables Across Specifications)")
print("─"*80)

key_vars = ["is_v5", "tv_is_inc", "tv_is_dec", "risk_ml",
            "cum_pos_rel_z", "cum_neg_rel_z", "dose_pos_rel_z", "dose_neg_rel_z"]
header = f"  {'Spec':<30}"
for v in key_vars:
    header += f" {v[:12]:>13}"
print(header)
print(f"  {'─'*140}")
for name, m in hazard_models.items():
    row = f"  {name[:30]:<30}"
    for v in key_vars:
        if v in m.params:
            c = m.params[v]; p = m.pvalues[v]
            row += f" {c:>+.3f}{stars(p):>4s}  "
        else:
            row += f" {'—':>13}"
    print(row)

# Store hazard evidence
if "H3: + HD risk control" in hazard_models:
    m_h3 = hazard_models["H3: + HD risk control"]
    EVIDENCE["hazard_regime_effect"] = m_h3.pvalues.get("is_v5", 1) < CFG["sig_threshold"]
    EVIDENCE["hazard_inc_effect"]    = m_h3.pvalues.get("tv_is_inc", 1) < CFG["sig_threshold"]
    EVIDENCE["hazard_regime_coef"]   = m_h3.params.get("is_v5", np.nan)
    EVIDENCE["hazard_inc_coef"]      = m_h3.params.get("tv_is_inc", np.nan)

print(f"\n  Hazard evidence (from H3, controlling for HD risk_ml):")
print(f"    Regime effect (is_v5):     {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('hazard_regime_effect'))}")
print(f"    Event-level inc effect:    {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('hazard_inc_effect'))}")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  F. TASK 4: EXTRA-INC PROPENSITY & MECHANISM TEST
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("  TASK 4: EXTRA-INC PROPENSITY & MECHANISM TEST")
print("="*80)

# ── F.1  First-stage model: P(inc | H, regime) ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  F.1  First-Stage: P(inc | H, regime) — Counterfactual Inc Probability")
print("  NOTE: delta_p_inc is a heuristic proxy, NOT a direct observation of")
print("  principal strata. It estimates how much V5 regime shifts each user's")
print("  P(inc) relative to V4 regime.")
print("─"*80)

fs_feats = [f for f in feat_inter_us if f in us.columns and us[f].notna().mean() > 0.3]
for extra in ["score_v4", "score_v5", "lc_z"]:
    if extra in us.columns and extra not in fs_feats:
        fs_feats.append(extra)

print(f"  First-stage features: {len(fs_feats)}")

us_fs = us.copy()
fs_feats_with_regime = fs_feats + ["is_v5"]
fs_feats_use = [f for f in fs_feats_with_regime if f in us_fs.columns]

ps_oof, ps_auc, ps_std, _ = xgb_crossfit(
    us_fs, fs_feats_use, "is_inc", CFG, group_col="user_id", mcw=5
)
us_fs["ps_inc"] = ps_oof
print(f"  Inc propensity model AUC: {ps_auc:.4f}±{ps_std:.4f}")

X_fs = us_fs[fs_feats_use].values.astype(np.float32)
y_fs = us_fs["is_inc"].values.astype(int)

params_fs = {
    "objective": "binary:logistic", "max_depth": 4, "learning_rate": 0.05,
    "subsample": 0.8, "colsample_bytree": 0.6, "min_child_weight": 5,
    "device": CFG["xgb_device"], "verbosity": 0, "seed": CFG["seed"],
}
dt_fs = xgb.DMatrix(X_fs, label=y_fs, feature_names=fs_feats_use, missing=np.nan)
bst_fs = xgb.train(params_fs, dt_fs, num_boost_round=300, verbose_eval=False)

is_v5_idx = fs_feats_use.index("is_v5")
X_cf_v4 = X_fs.copy(); X_cf_v4[:, is_v5_idx] = 0
X_cf_v5 = X_fs.copy(); X_cf_v5[:, is_v5_idx] = 1

us_fs["p_inc_if_v4"] = bst_fs.predict(xgb.DMatrix(X_cf_v4, feature_names=fs_feats_use, missing=np.nan))
us_fs["p_inc_if_v5"] = bst_fs.predict(xgb.DMatrix(X_cf_v5, feature_names=fs_feats_use, missing=np.nan))
us_fs["delta_p_inc"] = us_fs["p_inc_if_v5"] - us_fs["p_inc_if_v4"]

us = us.merge(us_fs[["user_id", "p_inc_if_v4", "p_inc_if_v5", "delta_p_inc"]],
              on="user_id", how="left", suffixes=("", "_fs"))

dp_mean = us["delta_p_inc"].mean()
dp_gt0  = (us["delta_p_inc"] > 0).mean()

print(f"\n  Counterfactual P(inc) summary:")
print(f"    P(inc | V4): mean={us['p_inc_if_v4'].mean():.4f}, sd={us['p_inc_if_v4'].std():.4f}")
print(f"    P(inc | V5): mean={us['p_inc_if_v5'].mean():.4f}, sd={us['p_inc_if_v5'].std():.4f}")
print(f"    Delta_p_inc: mean={dp_mean:+.4f}, sd={us['delta_p_inc'].std():.4f}")
print(f"    Delta_p_inc > 0: {dp_gt0:.1%}")
print(f"\n  INTERPRETATION:")
print(f"  delta_p_inc > 0 for {dp_gt0:.0%} of users — this is a near-universal upward shift,")
print(f"  NOT a cleanly separated local subgroup.  The proxy suggests V5 broadly raises")
print(f"  P(inc) for almost everyone, rather than targeting a specific subpopulation.")
print(f"  Principal strata (always-inc / never-inc / compliers) are NOT directly observed.")

# ── F.2  Delta_p_inc distribution & profiling ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  F.2  Delta_p_inc Distribution & Top-Quantile Profile")
print("─"*80)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))

ax = axes[0]
ax.hist(us["delta_p_inc"].dropna(), bins=50, alpha=0.7, color="C1", edgecolor="white")
ax.axvline(0, color="k", ls="--", lw=1)
ax.axvline(dp_mean, color="C3", ls="-", lw=2, label=f"mean={dp_mean:+.4f}")
ax.set(xlabel="Delta_p_inc (V5 - V4)", ylabel="Count",
       title=f"Distribution of Delta_p_inc\n({dp_gt0:.0%} > 0 — near-universal shift)")
ax.legend()

ax = axes[1]
us["dp_q4"] = pd.qcut(us["delta_p_inc"].rank(method="first"), q=4,
                        labels=["Q1 (low)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4 (high)"])
q_dr = us.groupby("dp_q4").agg(
    dr=("defaulted", "mean"), n=("defaulted", "count"), inc_rate=("is_inc", "mean"),
).reset_index()
colors = ["C0", "C2", "C1", "C3"]
ax.bar(range(len(q_dr)), q_dr["dr"] * 100, color=colors, alpha=0.7)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(q_dr)))
ax.set_xticklabels(q_dr["dp_q4"], fontsize=8)
for i, (r, n) in enumerate(zip(q_dr["dr"], q_dr["n"])):
    ax.text(i, r * 100 + 0.3, f"{r:.1%}\n(n={n})", ha="center", fontsize=7)
ax.set(ylabel="Default Rate (%)", title="Default Rate by Delta_p_inc Quartile")

ax = axes[2]
ax.scatter(us.loc[us["defaulted"] == 0, "delta_p_inc"],
           us.loc[us["defaulted"] == 0, "score_v4"], s=5, alpha=0.1, c="C7", label="Non-default")
ax.scatter(us.loc[us["defaulted"] == 1, "delta_p_inc"],
           us.loc[us["defaulted"] == 1, "score_v4"], s=15, alpha=0.6, c="C3", label="Default")
ax.set(xlabel="Delta_p_inc", ylabel="V4 Score", title="Defaulters in Delta_p × Score Space")
ax.legend()
fig.suptitle("Task 4: Extra-Inc Propensity Proxy (Delta_p_inc)", fontsize=14)
fig.tight_layout(); plt.show()

print("\n  Top-quartile (Q4) vs bottom-quartile (Q1) user profile:")
q4 = us[us["dp_q4"] == "Q4 (high)"]
q1 = us[us["dp_q4"] == "Q1 (low)"]
profile_vars = ["score_v4", "score_v5", CFG["col_bf_credit"], "delta_abs", "is_inc", "defaulted"]
print(f"  {'Variable':<25} {'Q4 (high Δp)':>15} {'Q1 (low Δp)':>15} {'Diff':>12}")
print(f"  {'─'*70}")
for v in profile_vars:
    if v in us.columns:
        m4 = q4[v].mean(); m1 = q1[v].mean()
        print(f"  {v:<25} {m4:>15.4f} {m1:>15.4f} {m4-m1:>+12.4f}")

# ── F.3  Hazard heterogeneity by delta_p_inc ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  F.3  Hazard Heterogeneity by Delta_p_inc")
print("─"*80)

cp = cp.merge(us[["user_id", "delta_p_inc", "dp_q4"]], on="user_id", how="left")
cp["delta_p_z"] = (cp["delta_p_inc"] - cp["delta_p_inc"].mean()) / cp["delta_p_inc"].std() \
    if cp["delta_p_inc"].std() > 0 else 0.0

# H7: + delta_p interaction with treatment
h7_tested = False
h7_int_coef = np.nan
h7_int_p    = np.nan
h7_int_ci   = (np.nan, np.nan)

try:
    m_h7 = smf.glm(
        "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml + delta_p_z + tv_is_inc:delta_p_z",
        data=cp.dropna(subset=["risk_ml", "delta_p_z"]),
        family=sm.families.Binomial()
    ).fit(cov_type="cluster", cov_kwds={"groups": cp.dropna(subset=["risk_ml", "delta_p_z"])["user_id"]})
    hazard_models["H7: + delta_p × inc interaction"] = m_h7
    stata_table(m_h7, "H7: Hazard with delta_p_inc × tv_is_inc interaction", show_or=True)

    h7_tested = True
    h7_int_coef = m_h7.params.get("tv_is_inc:delta_p_z", np.nan)
    h7_int_p    = m_h7.pvalues.get("tv_is_inc:delta_p_z", np.nan)
    ci_h7 = m_h7.conf_int()
    if "tv_is_inc:delta_p_z" in ci_h7.index:
        h7_int_ci = (ci_h7.loc["tv_is_inc:delta_p_z", 0], ci_h7.loc["tv_is_inc:delta_p_z", 1])
except Exception as e:
    print(f"  ERROR in H7: {e}")

# Determine local effect evidence from ACTUAL results
if h7_tested:
    h7_significant  = h7_int_p < CFG["sig_threshold"]
    h7_positive_dir = h7_int_coef > 0
    ci_spans_zero   = h7_int_ci[0] <= 0 <= h7_int_ci[1]

    if h7_significant and h7_positive_dir:
        local_effect_verdict = "Supported"
    elif h7_significant and not h7_positive_dir:
        local_effect_verdict = "Supported (opposite direction: weaker for high-delta_p)"
    elif not h7_significant:
        local_effect_verdict = "Not supported"
    else:
        local_effect_verdict = "Inconclusive"

    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_tested"]     = True
    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_supported"]  = h7_significant and h7_positive_dir
    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_verdict"]    = local_effect_verdict

    print(f"\n  ── H7 Interaction Evidence Assessment ──")
    print(f"  tv_is_inc × delta_p_z: coef={h7_int_coef:+.4f}, p={h7_int_p:.4f} {stars(h7_int_p)}")
    print(f"  95% CI: [{h7_int_ci[0]:+.4f}, {h7_int_ci[1]:+.4f}]")
    print(f"  CI spans zero: {'Yes' if ci_spans_zero else 'No'}")
    print(f"  Verdict: {local_effect_verdict}")

    if not h7_significant:
        print(f"  → No evidence that the inc effect on hazard is stronger for users")
        print(f"    with higher delta_p_inc.  The interaction is not significant.")
    elif h7_significant and h7_positive_dir:
        print(f"  → Evidence supports: inc effect is stronger for high-delta_p users.")
    elif h7_significant and not h7_positive_dir:
        print(f"  → Evidence suggests inc effect is WEAKER for high-delta_p users")
        print(f"    (opposite to the hypothesised direction).")
else:
    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_tested"]     = False
    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_supported"]  = False
    EVIDENCE["local_inc_effect_verdict"]    = "Not tested"

# H8: stratified — high vs low delta_p
for stratum, label in [("Q4 (high)", "High delta_p (Q4)"), ("Q1 (low)", "Low delta_p (Q1)")]:
    cp_sub = cp[cp["dp_q4"] == stratum].dropna(subset=["risk_ml"])
    if len(cp_sub) < 50 or cp_sub["event"].sum() < 5:
        print(f"\n  SKIP stratified {label}: too few obs/events")
        continue
    try:
        m_strat = smf.glm(
            "event ~ t_mid + t_mid_sq + is_v5 + tv_is_inc + tv_is_dec + risk_ml",
            data=cp_sub, family=sm.families.Binomial()
        ).fit(cov_type="cluster", cov_kwds={"groups": cp_sub["user_id"]})
        hazard_models[f"H8-{label}"] = m_strat
        stata_table(m_strat, f"H8 Stratified: {label}", show_or=True)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ERROR in stratified {label}: {e}")

# ── F.4  Mechanism Hypothesis Test ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  F.4  Mechanism Hypothesis Test")
print("  H: V5 score shift → mechanical inc increase → higher hazard")
print("  Each link is tested separately; the chain is NOT confirmed unless ALL hold.")
print("─"*80)

# [Link 1] Score distribution / calibration shift — from actual tests in D.1
print("\n  [Link 1] Score distribution / calibration shift:")
for sc in ["score_v4", "score_v5"]:
    r = score_shift_tests[sc]
    print(f"    {sc}: V4 mean={r['v4_mean']:.4f}, V5 mean={r['v5_mean']:.4f}, "
          f"diff={r['diff']:+.4f}, KS p={r['ks_p']:.4f} {stars(r['ks_p'])}, "
          f"t p={r['t_p']:.4f} {stars(r['t_p'])}")

# Derive evidence from tests, not hardcoded
# We require BOTH KS significant AND mean difference in expected direction
sv4_shift_sig = score_shift_tests["score_v4"]["ks_p"] < CFG["sig_threshold"]
sv5_shift_sig = score_shift_tests["score_v5"]["ks_p"] < CFG["sig_threshold"]
EVIDENCE["link1_score_distribution_shift"] = sv4_shift_sig or sv5_shift_sig

# Calibration shift (from D.4)
print(f"    Calibration shift evidence: {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('score_calibration_shift'))}")
print(f"    Distribution shift evidence: {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['link1_score_distribution_shift'])}")

# [Link 2] Rule mapping — from actual D.3 decomposition
print(f"\n  [Link 2] Rule mapping change (from Task 2 decomposition):")
print(f"    Level shift (regime main effect): {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_level_shift'))}")
if m_rule_primary is not None:
    print(f"      coef={rule_level_coef:+.4f}, p={rule_level_p:.4f} {stars(rule_level_p)}")
print(f"    Slope difference (interaction):   {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_slope_difference'))}")
if m_rule_primary is not None:
    print(f"      coef={rule_slope_coef:+.4f}, p={rule_slope_p:.4f} {stars(rule_slope_p)}")
print(f"    Any mapping change:               {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_any_change'))}")

# Proper characterisation
if EVIDENCE.get("mapping_level_shift") and not EVIDENCE.get("mapping_slope_difference"):
    EVIDENCE["link2_characterisation"] = "Level shift without slope change — parallel upward shift in P(inc)"
elif EVIDENCE.get("mapping_level_shift") and EVIDENCE.get("mapping_slope_difference"):
    EVIDENCE["link2_characterisation"] = "Both level and slope changed — full mapping restructuring"
elif not EVIDENCE.get("mapping_level_shift") and not EVIDENCE.get("mapping_slope_difference"):
    EVIDENCE["link2_characterisation"] = "Neither significant — consistent with (but not proof of) stable mapping"
else:
    EVIDENCE["link2_characterisation"] = "Slope change without level shift — unusual pattern"
print(f"    Characterisation: {EVIDENCE['link2_characterisation']}")

# [Link 3] V5 pushes more inc
print(f"\n  [Link 3] V5 pushes more users toward inc:")
print(f"    V4 inc rate = {inc_v4:.1%}, V5 inc rate = {inc_v5:.1%}, diff = {inc_v5-inc_v4:+.1%}")
EVIDENCE["link3_v5_more_inc"] = inc_v5 > inc_v4
print(f"    Verdict: {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['link3_v5_more_inc'])}")

# [Link 4] Inc effect stronger for high-delta_p users
print(f"\n  [Link 4] Inc effect on hazard stronger for high-delta_p users:")
print(f"    H7 interaction tested: {EVIDENCE['local_inc_effect_tested']}")
print(f"    Verdict: {EVIDENCE['local_inc_effect_verdict']}")
if h7_tested:
    print(f"    (coef={h7_int_coef:+.4f}, p={h7_int_p:.4f}, CI=[{h7_int_ci[0]:+.4f}, {h7_int_ci[1]:+.4f}])")

# ── F.5  Mechanism Summary — EVIDENCE-TIER LABELS ──
print("\n" + "─"*80)
print("  F.5  Mechanism Hypothesis Summary — Evidence-Tier Assessment")
print("  Labels: Supported | Partially supported | Not supported | Not tested")
print("─"*80)

# Determine chain-level verdict
chain_links = {
    "Link 1: Score distribution/calibration shift": EVIDENCE.get("link1_score_distribution_shift"),
    "Link 2: Rule mapping change":                  EVIDENCE.get("mapping_any_change"),
    "Link 3: V5 pushes more inc":                   EVIDENCE.get("link3_v5_more_inc"),
    "Link 4: Local inc effect for high-delta_p":     EVIDENCE.get("local_inc_effect_supported", False),
}

n_supported  = sum(1 for v in chain_links.values() if v is True)
n_not_supported = sum(1 for v in chain_links.values() if v is False)

print(f"\n  {'Hypothesis component':<55} {'Verdict':<25}")
print(f"  {'─'*80}")
for desc, flag in chain_links.items():
    if flag is True:
        lbl = "Supported"
    elif flag is False:
        lbl = "Not supported"
    elif flag is None:
        lbl = "Inconclusive"
    else:
        lbl = "Not tested"
    print(f"  {desc:<55} {lbl:<25}")

# Rule mapping nuance
print(f"\n  Rule mapping detail:")
print(f"    {EVIDENCE.get('link2_characterisation', 'N/A')}")

# Delta_p characterisation
print(f"\n  Delta_p_inc characterisation:")
print(f"    Mean = {dp_mean:+.4f}, {dp_gt0:.0%} of users have delta_p > 0")
print(f"    This is a near-universal upward shift, NOT a cleanly separated subgroup.")
print(f"    Principal strata are NOT directly identified.")

# Full chain verdict
print(f"\n  ── Full Chain Verdict ──")
print(f"  Hypothesis: 'score shift → more inc → more risk'")
if n_supported == 4:
    print(f"  Status: All links supported — chain is consistent with evidence.")
    print(f"          But this does NOT constitute causal confirmation of the full chain.")
elif n_supported >= 2 and not chain_links.get("Link 4: Local inc effect for high-delta_p"):
    print(f"  Status: Partially supported ({n_supported}/4 links hold).")
    print(f"          The upstream links (score shift, more inc) may hold,")
    print(f"          but the critical local-effect link (Link 4) is NOT supported.")
    print(f"          The full chain cannot be confirmed.")
else:
    print(f"  Status: Not established ({n_supported}/4 links hold).")
    print(f"          Insufficient evidence to confirm the proposed mechanism.")

print(f"\n  Current hazard results more directly support:")
print(f"    - Regime effect / event-level risk marking / targeting signal")
print(f"    - NOT necessarily a clean 'extra inc → extra risk' local causal path")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#  G. FINAL SUMMARY — EVIDENCE-TIER STYLE
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "═"*80)
print("  FINAL SUMMARY — EVIDENCE-TIER ASSESSMENT")
print("═"*80)

print(f"""
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 1: PERFORMATIVE PREDICTION (PfP)                                │
  │  All PfP models control for m(X) = risk_ml                            │
  │  (cross-fitted XGB on {n_feat_risk} HD features, AUC={risk_auc_us:.4f})              │
  │  + baseline credit (lc_z).                                            │
  │                                                                        │
  │  V4 score residual signal beyond m(X): {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['pfp_v4_residual_signal']):<20s}          │
  │  V5 score residual signal beyond m(X): {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['pfp_v5_residual_signal']):<20s}          │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Interpretation: score not fully absorbed by X;                        │
  │  consistent with PfP content but NOT causal proof.                    │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
""")

print(f"""
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 2: RULE / POLICY MAPPING                                        │
  │  Score distribution shift:  {evidence_label(EVIDENCE['any_score_distribution_shift']):<20s}                       │
  │  Calibration shift:         {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('score_calibration_shift')):<20s}                       │
  │  Mapping level shift:       {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_level_shift')):<20s}                       │
  │  Mapping slope difference:  {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_slope_difference')):<20s}                       │
  │  V5 higher inc rate:        {'Yes' if EVIDENCE['v5_higher_inc_rate'] else 'No':<20s}                       │
  │                                                                        │
  │  {EVIDENCE.get('link2_characterisation', 'N/A'):<70s}│
  │                                                                        │
  │  NOTE: 'interaction not significant' ≠ 'mapping stable'.              │
  │  If level shift IS significant, mapping has changed even              │
  │  with the same slope.                                                  │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
""")

print(f"""
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 3: HAZARD (Main Analysis)                                       │
  │  Regime effect (is_v5):    {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('hazard_regime_effect')):<20s}                       │
  │  Event-level inc effect:   {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('hazard_inc_effect')):<20s}                       │
  │  Cumulative/dose exposure: see H5/H6 in table above                  │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Sample = selected continuing users — do NOT extrapolate.             │
  │  Regime effect ≠ pure treatment effect; it is a joint shock.          │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
""")

print(f"""
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 4: EXTRA-INC PROPENSITY & MECHANISM                             │
  │  Constructed delta_p_inc proxy:                                        │
  │    mean = {dp_mean:+.4f}, proportion > 0 = {dp_gt0:.0%}                              │
  │    This is a near-universal upward shift, NOT a clean subgroup.       │
  │    Principal strata are NOT directly observed.                        │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Mechanism chain 'score shift → more inc → more risk':                │
  │    Link 1 (score shift):        {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('link1_score_distribution_shift')):<20s}                │
  │    Link 2 (mapping change):     {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('mapping_any_change')):<20s}                │
  │    Link 3 (V5 more inc):        {evidence_label(EVIDENCE.get('link3_v5_more_inc')):<20s}                │
  │    Link 4 (local inc effect):   {EVIDENCE.get('local_inc_effect_verdict', 'Not tested'):<20s}                │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Full chain status: NOT established.                                  │
  │  The critical Link 4 (local effect for high-delta_p users)            │
  │  is {EVIDENCE.get('local_inc_effect_verdict', 'not tested').lower()}.  Current hazard results more directly   │
  │  support regime effect / event-level risk marking.                    │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
""")

print(f"""
  Identification boundaries:
  ─────────────────────────
  - Maintained assumption: Y(a) ⊥ A_t | X_t  (sequential ignorability)
  - Treatment defined at event level (tv_is_inc), with cumulative exposure
    as supplementary measure (cum_pos_rel, dose_pos_rel)
  - Sample = selected continuing users (pre-shock + overlap window)
  - Default proxy: event_date ≈ default_date (label is back-filled)
  - Principal stratum NOT directly observed; delta_p_inc is a heuristic proxy
    showing near-universal upward shift, not a cleanly separated subgroup
  - Regime effect ≠ pure treatment effect; it is a joint model-policy shock
  - All exposure variables standardised; coefficients are per-SD effects
  - This notebook does NOT claim:
      ✗ Clean identification of inc causal effect
      ✗ Confirmation of the full mechanism chain
      ✗ That rule mapping is stable (even if interaction is not significant)
      ✗ That extra-inc users have been identified as a principal stratum

{'═'*80}
  Analysis complete.
{'═'*80}
""")

================================================================================
  SECTION B: DATA LOADING & PREPROCESSING
================================================================================
  Overlap window : 2025-03-20 00:00:00 to 2025-06-05 00:00:00
  Users          : 2101, V4=1523, V5=578
  Defaults       : 208 (9.9%)
  Treatment      : inc=1436, same=181, dec=484
  User-level risk score (X_inter, 803 features): AUC=0.5109±0.0162

  ── m(X) definition for all subsequent PfP / control analyses ──
  m(X) = risk_ml: a cross-fitted XGBoost prediction of 'defaulted'
         using 803 features from feat_inter (V4 ∩ V5 feature set),
         trained via 5-fold GroupKFold (grouped by user_id).
         OOF AUC = 0.5109±0.0162
  This is the HIGH-DIMENSIONAL baseline control used throughout.
  When we say 'controlling for m(X)', we mean including risk_ml as a covariate.
  risk_ml captures the observable X's predictive content for default,
  but it is NOT the same as the V4/V5 model scores (score_v4, score_v5).
  The PfP test asks: after absorbing m(X), do score_v4/v5 still add signal?

================================================================================
  TASK 1: PERFORMATIVE PREDICTION (PfP) TEST
  Goal: Does model score carry additional predictive signal beyond m(X)?
================================================================================

  ── What is m(X) here? ──
  m(X) = risk_ml, a cross-fitted XGBoost model of default using 803
  HD features from the V4 ∩ V5 intersection feature set.
  OOF AUC = 0.5109.  It represents the best prediction of default
  achievable from the observable covariates X alone.

  The PfP question is: after controlling for m(X) + baseline credit (lc_z),
  does the V4 or V5 model score still carry significant residual signal
  for default?  If yes, the score contains content NOT fully absorbed by X,
  which is consistent with (but does not prove) performative / policy-feedback.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  C.1  GLM Logistic Regression: score → default | m(X)
  NOTE: All models below control for m(X) = risk_ml + lc_z.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-0: defaulted ~ risk_ml + lc_z  [Baseline: m(X) only, no score]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.1762     0.1208   -18.01   0.0000  [ -2.4130,  -1.9394] ***
  risk_ml                        -0.5869     1.3575    -0.43   0.6655  [ -3.2475,   2.0738] 
  lc_z                           -0.1585     0.0699    -2.27   0.0233  [ -0.2955,  -0.0216] **
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 1358.2
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-1: defaulted ~ score_v4_z + m(X)  [Add V4 score to baseline]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.1701     0.1215   -17.86   0.0000  [ -2.4082,  -1.9320] ***
  score_v4_z                      0.1305     0.0755     1.73   0.0841  [ -0.0176,   0.2785] *
  risk_ml                        -0.7472     1.3566    -0.55   0.5818  [ -3.4061,   1.9116] 
  lc_z                           -0.0998     0.0788    -1.27   0.2053  [ -0.2543,   0.0547] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 1357.5
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-2: defaulted ~ score_v5_z + m(X)  [Add V5 score to baseline]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.1623     0.1224   -17.67   0.0000  [ -2.4022,  -1.9225] ***
  score_v5_z                      0.2129     0.0747     2.85   0.0044  [  0.0665,   0.3593] ***
  risk_ml                        -0.9818     1.3803    -0.71   0.4769  [ -3.6871,   1.7234] 
  lc_z                           -0.0609     0.0807    -0.76   0.4502  [ -0.2190,   0.0972] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 1353.0
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-3: defaulted ~ score_v4_z + score_v5_z + m(X)  [Both scores]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.1620     0.1223   -17.68   0.0000  [ -2.4016,  -1.9223] ***
  score_v4_z                     -0.0810     0.1282    -0.63   0.5271  [ -0.3322,   0.1701] 
  score_v5_z                      0.2745     0.1243     2.21   0.0272  [  0.0308,   0.5182] **
  risk_ml                        -0.9990     1.3859    -0.72   0.4710  [ -3.7154,   1.7173] 
  lc_z                           -0.0698     0.0821    -0.85   0.3950  [ -0.2307,   0.0910] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 1354.6
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-4: + score × regime interactions + m(X)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.1878     0.1279   -17.11   0.0000  [ -2.4384,  -1.9372] ***
  score_v4_z                     -0.0966     0.1526    -0.63   0.5267  [ -0.3957,   0.2025] 
  score_v5_z                      0.2499     0.1419     1.76   0.0782  [ -0.0282,   0.5279] *
  score_v4_z:is_v5                0.0023     0.2808     0.01   0.9935  [ -0.5481,   0.5527] 
  score_v5_z:is_v5                0.1180     0.2854     0.41   0.6792  [ -0.4413,   0.6774] 
  is_v5                           0.0723     0.1653     0.44   0.6618  [ -0.2517,   0.3963] 
  risk_ml                        -0.9659     1.3812    -0.70   0.4843  [ -3.6730,   1.7412] 
  lc_z                           -0.0747     0.0825    -0.91   0.3653  [ -0.2365,   0.0871] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 1359.6
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-5: Subsample Anchor=V4 only + m(X)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -2.2622     0.1427   -15.86   0.0000  [ -2.5418,  -1.9826] ***
  score_v4_z                     -0.0944     0.1531    -0.62   0.5374  [ -0.3945,   0.2056] 
  score_v5_z                      0.2421     0.1421     1.70   0.0885  [ -0.0365,   0.5207] *
  risk_ml                         0.0890     1.5725     0.06   0.9549  [ -2.9930,   3.1711] 
  lc_z                           -0.0706     0.0989    -0.71   0.4754  [ -0.2645,   0.1233] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 1,523   AIC = 970.3
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model PfP-6: Subsample Anchor=V5 only + m(X)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -1.9259     0.2321    -8.30   0.0000  [ -2.3808,  -1.4710] ***
  score_v4_z                     -0.0970     0.2380    -0.41   0.6837  [ -0.5634,   0.3695] 
  score_v5_z                      0.3826     0.2556     1.50   0.1344  [ -0.1184,   0.8837] 
  risk_ml                        -3.7876     2.8397    -1.33   0.1823  [ -9.3534,   1.7781] 
  lc_z                           -0.0729     0.1504    -0.49   0.6276  [ -0.3677,   0.2218] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 578   AIC = 392.0
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  C.2  DML / Partialling-out: residualised score → residualised default
  Procedure: E[Y|X] via risk_ml; E[score|X] via cross-fitted XGB regression;
  then OLS of Y-residual on score-residual.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  DML result for score_v4 (controlling for m(X) via partialling-out):
