  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Rep         Theta         SE          p
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  0         0.06368    0.05919     0.2820 
  1         0.07823    0.05918     0.1862 
  2         0.07701    0.05962     0.1965 
  3         0.07020    0.05933     0.2367 
  4         0.07305    0.06005     0.2238 
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Median theta = 0.07305, Median SE = 0.05933
  Cauchy-combined p = 0.2211 

  DML result for score_v5 (controlling for m(X) via partialling-out):
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Rep         Theta         SE          p
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  0         0.11056    0.05973     0.0642 *
  1         0.10687    0.05947     0.0723 *
  2         0.10745    0.05890     0.0681 *
  3         0.10282    0.06014     0.0873 *
  4         0.11452    0.05962     0.0548 *
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Median theta = 0.10745, Median SE = 0.05962
  Cauchy-combined p = 0.0678 *

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  C.3  PfP Summary Table
  All models control for m(X) = risk_ml (cross-fitted HD risk score) + lc_z
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Model                                      score_v4_z   score_v5_z        AIC
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  PfP-0: m(X) only [baseline]                         —            —     1358.2
  PfP-1: + v4_score | m(X)                     +0.1305*            —     1357.5
  PfP-2: + v5_score | m(X)                            —   +0.2129***     1353.0
  PfP-3: + both scores | m(X)                   -0.0810    +0.2745**     1354.6
  PfP-4: + score × regime | m(X)                -0.0966     +0.2499*     1359.6

  PfP evidence flags (derived from PfP-3, controlling for m(X)):
    score_v4 carries residual signal beyond m(X): Not supported
    score_v5 carries residual signal beyond m(X): Supported

  Interpretation:
  - 'Controlling for m(X)' means: risk_ml (cross-fitted XGB, 803 features) + lc_z
  - If score is significant here, it means score contains signal that the
    observable X alone cannot reproduce.  This is consistent with PfP content
    (score carries information about treatment/policy, not just risk).
  - This does NOT establish causal PfP.  It only shows the score is not
    a pure sufficient statistic of X for predicting default.

================================================================================
  TASK 2: RULE / POLICY MAPPING TEST
  Goal: Given same 'score position', does P(inc) differ across V4/V5?
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.1  Score Distribution Comparison: V4 vs V5
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Score distribution tests:
  Score           KS stat       KS p     t stat        t p    V4 mean    V5 mean       Diff
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  score_v4     0.0175      0.0175         -2.89   0.0039     0.2586     0.2747   +0.0161 ***
  score_v5     0.1748      0.1748         -1.64   0.1004     0.2164     0.2256   +0.0092 

  Evidence flags (from actual KS + t-tests, threshold p < 0.1):
    V4-score distribution differs across regimes: Supported
    V5-score distribution differs across regimes: Not supported

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.2  P(inc | score_position, regime) — Three Scales
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.3  Logistic: inc ~ score + regime + score × regime
  Decomposition: regime main effect = LEVEL SHIFT
                 interaction         = SLOPE DIFFERENCE
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Rule Mapping [V4 Z-score]: inc ~ score + regime + interaction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                       0.6541     0.0550    11.88   0.0000  [  0.5462,   0.7620] ***
  _sc                            -0.4083     0.0562    -7.26   0.0000  [ -0.5186,  -0.2981] ***
  is_v5                           0.5664     0.1166     4.86   0.0000  [  0.3378,   0.7950] ***
  _sc:is_v5                      -0.0339     0.1049    -0.32   0.7464  [ -0.2396,   0.1717] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 2534.0
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01
  ── Decomposition for V4 Z-score ──
     Level shift (regime main effect is_v5):  coef=+0.5664, p=0.0000 ***
     Slope difference (score × regime):       coef=-0.0339, p=0.7464 
     → Evidence of LEVEL SHIFT: at the same score position, V5 has a
       higher baseline P(inc), regardless of score.
     → No significant slope difference detected at p < 0.1.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Rule Mapping [V4 Percentile]: inc ~ score + regime + interaction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                       1.3473     0.1164    11.57   0.0000  [  1.1191,   1.5755] ***
  _sc                            -1.3760     0.1953    -7.05   0.0000  [ -1.7588,  -0.9932] ***
  is_v5                           0.6722     0.2538     2.65   0.0081  [  0.1748,   1.1697] ***
  _sc:is_v5                      -0.2255     0.3928    -0.57   0.5660  [ -0.9953,   0.5444] 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 2538.2
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01
  ── Decomposition for V4 Percentile ──
     Level shift (regime main effect is_v5):  coef=+0.6722, p=0.0081 ***
     Slope difference (score × regime):       coef=-0.2255, p=0.5660 
     → Evidence of LEVEL SHIFT: at the same score position, V5 has a
       higher baseline P(inc), regardless of score.
     → No significant slope difference detected at p < 0.1.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Rule Mapping [Within-model Z-score]: inc ~ score + regime + interaction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                       0.6699     0.0552    12.13   0.0000  [  0.5617,   0.7782] ***
  _sc                            -0.3957     0.0545    -7.26   0.0000  [ -0.5025,  -0.2889] ***
  is_v5                           0.5405     0.1165     4.64   0.0000  [  0.3122,   0.7688] ***
  _sc:is_v5                      -0.2212     0.1119    -1.98   0.0480  [ -0.4405,  -0.0019] **
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 2,101   AIC = 2518.4
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01
  ── Decomposition for Within-model Z-score ──
     Level shift (regime main effect is_v5):  coef=+0.5405, p=0.0000 ***
     Slope difference (score × regime):       coef=-0.2212, p=0.0480 **
     → Evidence of LEVEL SHIFT: at the same score position, V5 has a
       higher baseline P(inc), regardless of score.
     → Evidence of SLOPE DIFFERENCE: the score-inc mapping has
       a different gradient under V5.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.4  Calibration Check: Actual Default Rate by Score Bin × Regime
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Mean calibration gap (score_v4): V5 - V4 actual DR at same score bin = +0.0079
  Mean calibration gap (score_v5): V5 - V4 actual DR at same score bin = +0.0063

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.5  Within-Percentile Inc Rate Comparison
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  D.6  Rule Mapping Evidence Summary
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Inc rate: V4=65.7%, V5=75.4%, diff=+9.8%

  ── Evidence Summary (Task 2) ──
  Score distribution shift:       Supported
    (V4 score across regimes: KS p=0.0175)
    (V5 score across regimes: KS p=0.1748)
  Calibration shift:              Not supported
  Mapping level shift (is_v5):    Supported
    (coef=+0.6722, p=0.0081)
  Mapping slope difference:       Not supported
    (coef=-0.2255, p=0.5660)
  V5 has higher inc rate:         Yes

  CRITICAL NOTE on interpretation:
  - 'Interaction not significant' does NOT mean 'rule mapping is stable'.
  - If the level shift (regime main effect) IS significant, the mapping
    has changed even if the slope is the same: P(inc) is shifted up/down
    at every score position.
  - To claim 'mapping stable', BOTH level AND slope must be non-significant.
  → Current evidence: no slope reshaping, but there IS a level shift.
    This means the rule mapping has changed (parallel upward shift),
    even though the score-to-inc gradient is similar across regimes.

================================================================================
  TASK 3: HAZARD MAIN ANALYSIS — Discrete-Time Pooled Logistic
  IMPORTANT: Sample = selected continuing users. Do NOT extrapolate.
================================================================================

================================================================================
  TASK 4: EXTRA-INC PROPENSITY & MECHANISM TEST
================================================================================

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  F.1  First-Stage: P(inc | H, regime) — Counterfactual Inc Probability
  NOTE: delta_p_inc is a heuristic proxy, NOT a direct observation of
  principal strata. It estimates how much V5 regime shifts each user's
  P(inc) relative to V4 regime.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  First-stage features: 715
  Inc propensity model AUC: 0.8380±0.0130

  Counterfactual P(inc) summary:
    P(inc | V4): mean=0.6672, sd=0.3562
    P(inc | V5): mean=0.7331, sd=0.3239
    Delta_p_inc: mean=+0.0659, sd=0.0742
    Delta_p_inc > 0: 95.1%

  INTERPRETATION:
  delta_p_inc > 0 for 95% of users — this is a near-universal upward shift,
  NOT a cleanly separated local subgroup.  The proxy suggests V5 broadly raises
  P(inc) for almost everyone, rather than targeting a specific subpopulation.
  Principal strata (always-inc / never-inc / compliers) are NOT directly observed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  F.2  Delta_p_inc Distribution & Top-Quantile Profile
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Top-quartile (Q4) vs bottom-quartile (Q1) user profile:
  Variable                     Q4 (high Δp)     Q1 (low Δp)         Diff
  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  score_v4                           0.2866          0.2155      +0.0711
  score_v5                           0.2317          0.1808      +0.0509
  first_bf_credit                60871.6571      48404.0684  +12467.5887
  delta_abs                        807.4095      24567.1293  -23759.7198
  is_inc                             0.2971          0.8745      -0.5774
  defaulted                          0.0743          0.0932      -0.0189

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  F.3  Hazard Heterogeneity by Delta_p_inc
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  H7: Hazard with delta_p_inc × tv_is_inc interaction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Variable                         Coef.   Std.Err.        z    P>|z|              [95% CI]       OR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Intercept                      -5.0377     0.2105   -23.94   0.0000  [ -5.4502,  -4.6253] ***    0.006
  t_mid                           0.0416     0.0139     2.99   0.0028  [  0.0144,   0.0689] ***    1.043
  t_mid_sq                       -0.0004     0.0003    -1.61   0.1066  [ -0.0009,   0.0001]     1.000
  is_v5                           0.4001     0.1614     2.48   0.0132  [  0.0838,   0.7165] **    1.492
  tv_is_inc                       0.3787     0.1795     2.11   0.0349  [  0.0269,   0.7306] **    1.460
  tv_is_dec                       0.4702     0.1982     2.37   0.0177  [  0.0817,   0.8587] **    1.600
  risk_ml                         8.7968     3.1627     2.78   0.0054  [  2.5980,  14.9957] ***  6613.346
  delta_p_z                      -0.1778     0.1004    -1.77   0.0766  [ -0.3746,   0.0190] *    0.837
  tv_is_inc:delta_p_z            -0.0138     0.1500    -0.09   0.9268  [ -0.3078,   0.2803]     0.986
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  N = 10,314   AIC = 2008.5
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

  ── H7 Interaction Evidence Assessment ──
  tv_is_inc × delta_p_z: coef=-0.0138, p=0.9268 
  95% CI: [-0.3078, +0.2803]
  CI spans zero: Yes
  Verdict: Not supported
  → No evidence that the inc effect on hazard is stronger for users
    with higher delta_p_inc.  The interaction is not significant.

  t_mid_sq                       -0.0007     0.0005    -1.33   0.1829  [ -0.0017,   0.0003]     0.999

  tv_is_inc                       0.3734     0.3726     1.00   0.3164  [ -0.3570,   1.1037]     1.453
  tv_is_dec                       0.5128     0.4176     1.23   0.2195  [ -0.3057,   1.3313]     1.670

  N = 2,622   AIC = 491.3
  * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  F.4  Mechanism Hypothesis Test
  H: V5 score shift → mechanical inc increase → higher hazard
  Each link is tested separately; the chain is NOT confirmed unless ALL hold.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  [Link 1] Score distribution / calibration shift:
    score_v4: V4 mean=0.2586, V5 mean=0.2747, diff=+0.0161, KS p=0.0175 **, t p=0.0039 ***
    score_v5: V4 mean=0.2164, V5 mean=0.2256, diff=+0.0092, KS p=0.1748 , t p=0.1004 
    Calibration shift evidence: Not supported
    Distribution shift evidence: Supported

  [Link 2] Rule mapping change (from Task 2 decomposition):
    Level shift (regime main effect): Supported
      coef=+0.6722, p=0.0081 ***
    Slope difference (interaction):   Not supported
      coef=-0.2255, p=0.5660 
    Any mapping change:               Supported
    Characterisation: Level shift without slope change — parallel upward shift in P(inc)

  [Link 3] V5 pushes more users toward inc:
    V4 inc rate = 65.7%, V5 inc rate = 75.4%, diff = +9.8%
    Verdict: Supported

  [Link 4] Inc effect on hazard stronger for high-delta_p users:
    H7 interaction tested: True
    Verdict: Not supported
    (coef=-0.0138, p=0.9268, CI=[-0.3078, +0.2803])

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  F.5  Mechanism Hypothesis Summary — Evidence-Tier Assessment
  Labels: Supported | Partially supported | Not supported | Not tested
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Hypothesis component                                    Verdict                  
  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Link 1: Score distribution/calibration shift            Not tested               
  Link 2: Rule mapping change                             Not tested               
  Link 3: V5 pushes more inc                              Not tested               
  Link 4: Local inc effect for high-delta_p               Not tested               

  Rule mapping detail:
    Level shift without slope change — parallel upward shift in P(inc)

  Delta_p_inc characterisation:
    Mean = +0.0659, 95% of users have delta_p > 0
    This is a near-universal upward shift, NOT a cleanly separated subgroup.
    Principal strata are NOT directly identified.

  ── Full Chain Verdict ──
  Hypothesis: 'score shift → more inc → more risk'
  Status: Not established (0/4 links hold).
          Insufficient evidence to confirm the proposed mechanism.

  Current hazard results more directly support:
    - Regime effect / event-level risk marking / targeting signal
    - NOT necessarily a clean 'extra inc → extra risk' local causal path

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  FINAL SUMMARY — EVIDENCE-TIER ASSESSMENT
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 1: PERFORMATIVE PREDICTION (PfP)                                │
  │  All PfP models control for m(X) = risk_ml                            │
  │  (cross-fitted XGB on 803 HD features, AUC=0.5109)              │
  │  + baseline credit (lc_z).                                            │
  │                                                                        │
  │  V4 score residual signal beyond m(X): Not supported                 │
  │  V5 score residual signal beyond m(X): Supported                     │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Interpretation: score not fully absorbed by X;                        │
  │  consistent with PfP content but NOT causal proof.                    │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 2: RULE / POLICY MAPPING                                        │
  │  Score distribution shift:  Supported                                  │
  │  Calibration shift:         Not supported                              │
  │  Mapping level shift:       Supported                                  │
  │  Mapping slope difference:  Not supported                              │
  │  V5 higher inc rate:        Yes                                        │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Level shift without slope change — parallel upward shift in P(inc)    │
  │                                                                        │
  │  NOTE: 'interaction not significant' ≠ 'mapping stable'.              │
  │  If level shift IS significant, mapping has changed even              │
  │  with the same slope.                                                  │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 3: HAZARD (Main Analysis)                                       │
  │  Regime effect (is_v5):    Supported                                  │
  │  Event-level inc effect:   Supported                                  │
  │  Cumulative/dose exposure: see H5/H6 in table above                  │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Sample = selected continuing users — do NOT extrapolate.             │
  │  Regime effect ≠ pure treatment effect; it is a joint shock.          │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  TASK 4: EXTRA-INC PROPENSITY & MECHANISM                             │
  │  Constructed delta_p_inc proxy:                                        │
  │    mean = +0.0659, proportion > 0 = 95%                              │
  │    This is a near-universal upward shift, NOT a clean subgroup.       │
  │    Principal strata are NOT directly observed.                        │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Mechanism chain 'score shift → more inc → more risk':                │
  │    Link 1 (score shift):        Supported                           │
  │    Link 2 (mapping change):     Supported                           │
  │    Link 3 (V5 more inc):        Supported                           │
  │    Link 4 (local inc effect):   Not supported                       │
  │                                                                        │
  │  Full chain status: NOT established.                                  │
  │  The critical Link 4 (local effect for high-delta_p users)            │
  │  is not supported.  Current hazard results more directly   │
  │  support regime effect / event-level risk marking.                    │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘


  Identification boundaries:
  ─────────────────────────
  - Maintained assumption: Y(a) ⊥ A_t | X_t  (sequential ignorability)
  - Treatment defined at event level (tv_is_inc), with cumulative exposure
    as supplementary measure (cum_pos_rel, dose_pos_rel)
  - Sample = selected continuing users (pre-shock + overlap window)
  - Default proxy: event_date ≈ default_date (label is back-filled)
  - Principal stratum NOT directly observed; delta_p_inc is a heuristic proxy
    showing near-universal upward shift, not a cleanly separated subgroup
  - Regime effect ≠ pure treatment effect; it is a joint model-policy shock
  - All exposure variables standardised; coefficients are per-SD effects
  - This notebook does NOT claim:
      ✗ Clean identification of inc causal effect
      ✗ Confirmation of the full mechanism chain
      ✗ That rule mapping is stable (even if interaction is not significant)
      ✗ That extra-inc users have been identified as a principal stratum

════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  Analysis complete.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
